hard.next_step: 1/05/2026 RH Kara New Year's message and connect request for 2026 initiative. Stalled due to IT resources committed to other projects.
hard.ais_why:
• Yurman procurement leads explicitly want to 'embed an AI-driven Teams assistant for conversational procurement tasks without system navigation' - a direct agentic-intake request.
• They also called out leveraging AI-enhanced OCR and GenAI for invoice capture targeting high-90s accuracy, showing GenAI is already in their requirements language.
• Brand-new indirect function under Kara Fisher means greenfield buy; one strong 32-min call plus active SF cadence puts AI fit at high confidence, even with IT-resource stall.

north_star_verdict.headline: Cold for 159 days since the 28 January 2026 last touch. Richard Hunsinger's 5 January 2026 re-engagement note went unanswered and the initiative is stalled on David Yurman IT resourcing. One strong discovery call, then silence, with no champion, no engaged economic buyer, and no named competitor.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2 October 2025, in Qualified since 29 October 2025 (about 250 days in stage as of 6 July 2026). Only buyer engagement is the single discovery call on 1 October 2025; last verified activity 28 January 2026 (159 days ago). Owner's 5 January 2026 New Year connect request drew no reply. Close date 28 February 2027 is 237 days out, so no date pressure, but 159 days of silence in early Qualified with a stated IT-resource block and no champion means the deal is cold and needs a full re-engagement to restart.
evidence: Next Step dated 5 January 2026: Richard Hunsinger sent Kara Fisher a New Year message and connect request, no buyer reply; explicitly stalled due to IT resources committed to other projects
Single discovery call 1 October 2025 (about 9 months ago), no follow-on buyer engagement
No champion identified; single-threaded to operational AP/procurement contacts with no engaged economic buyer or decision maker
Stage Qualified for about 250 days with no progression

metrics.narrative: David Yurman articulated clear operational pain on the 1 October 2025 discovery call but never quantified it. Kara Fisher (Director of Indirect Procurement) said the indirect-procurement function she stood up 8 months earlier is fully manual and building from the ground up, aiming for a procure-to-pay system with finance. Julio Rivera (Senior AP Manager) said their 2021 tool is clunky and missing standard functions like delegation and out-of-office routing, forcing AP staff to 'become more AP finance personnel than IT troubleshoot personnel.' No savings target, headcount, cycle-time, or error-rate figure was stated, and the buyer declined to share spend scope citing no NDA. Quantifying the business case with finance is the move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Kara Fisher
Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera

economic_buyer.narrative: Christopher Clipper (Chief Financial Officer) is listed as Executive Sponsor on the Salesforce opportunity contact role, so the economic buyer is mapped at CFO level, but he has never appeared on a call or engaged in this cycle. The single 1 October 2025 discovery call was operational only: Kara Fisher, Julio Rivera, and Carrie (AP Supervisor). Julio framed the initiative as a procurement-and-finance partnership and referenced budget but declined specifics without an NDA, so budget authority is named but unvalidated and no commercial conversation has occurred. Reaching Clipper through Kara Fisher to confirm this is a funded 2026 priority is the move.
sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: Christopher Clipper, CFO, Executive Sponsor
Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025: operational attendees only, no CFO present

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated concrete evaluation criteria on the 1 October 2025 call even though no formal RFP or scoring is documented. Julio Rivera named ease of use, product maturity with standard features (delegation, out-of-office, parallel approvals), real-time workflow connected to their ERP, vendor onboarding and a vendor portal, and a payment step that stays in their bank-integrated ERP. Kara Fisher added a centralized, Amazon-style catalog to control purchasing across 50-plus retail stores. Julio also probed AI specifics, asking how the Teams conversational agent is audited and confirming interest in pure-AI invoice capture. Criteria are confirmed at the requirements level; formal weighting and a written BRD are not yet in evidence.
sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera
Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Kara Fisher

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is not mapped. The only session was operational discovery on 1 October 2025; Julio Rivera said they are 'talking to quite a few vendors' and would 'check back in with Kara and internally,' implying an internal evaluation but naming no approval chain, timeline, or selection milestone. The 5 January 2026 Next Step shows the initiative paused because David Yurman IT resources were pulled to other projects, so IT capacity appears to gate progress, but whether IT is a formal approval step is unknown. Mapping how a purchase gets approved, and who beyond the CFO signs off, is the move once the relationship reopens.
sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera
Next Step 5 January 2026: stalled due to IT resources committed to other projects

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unmapped, which is expected in early Qualified. The 1 October 2025 call never reached commercials; the buyer declined to share spend scope because no NDA is in place. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, InfoSec questionnaire, legal review, or implementation partner has been discussed. Signing an NDA is the first paper step and would unlock the spend-scope and business-case conversation; the rest of the paper process becomes visible only after the buyer re-engages commercially.
sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025: NDA not signed, buyer declined spend-scope questions

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and owned. On the 1 October 2025 call Kara Fisher described a brand-new indirect-procurement function, 8 months old, fully manual, with purchasing a 'free for all' across 50-plus retail stores and a need to centralize spend and enforce uniformity amid rapid growth. Julio Rivera described their 2021 tool as clunky and non-intuitive, missing expected-standard features (delegation, out-of-office routing, parallel approvals, real-time ERP-connected workflow, vendor portal), causing user-adoption failure and diverting AP staff to IT troubleshooting. The pain owners are Kara Fisher and Julio Rivera. What is missing is a quantified financial impact and CFO validation that this pain is a funded priority.
sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Kara Fisher
Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Kara Fisher (Director of Indirect Procurement) coordinated and led the 1 October 2025 discovery call and articulated the vision, positioning her as a possible developing champion, but she has shown no advocacy, made no internal introductions, and not replied to Richard Hunsinger's 5 January 2026 connect request; there has been no engagement for 159 days. What developed is a credible operational relationship and a clear pain narrative; what is missing is any evidence she will advocate internally or secure prioritization, especially against the stated IT-resource constraint. The move is to re-engage Kara with a value trigger to test whether she can be reactivated, and to build a second thread in parallel.
sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025: Kara Fisher led the meeting, operational engagement only
Next Step 5 January 2026: connect request to Kara, no reply, stalled on IT resources

competition.narrative: The competitive field is unknown. No competitor was named by the buyer on the 1 October 2025 call and the Salesforce Competitors field is empty. Julio Rivera said they are 'talking to quite a few vendors' and 'want to know what makes you different and whether it makes sense for our match,' confirming an active multi-vendor evaluation but disclosing no names or shortlist. This is a greenfield buy replacing a clunky in-house 2021 indirect tool, not a sole-source; after 159 days of silence Zycus may have been deprioritized without notice. Directly surfacing who else is being evaluated and where Zycus stands is the move on re-engagement.
sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera: talking to multiple vendors, none named

deal_movement.summary: One discovery call on 1 October 2025, then a stage bump to Qualified and a close-date push, then silence. The only touch in 2026 is Richard Hunsinger's unanswered 5 January New Year connect request; last activity 28 January 2026. The deal has been buyer-blocked on IT resourcing since.

items.change: Discovery call held with Kara Fisher, Julio Rivera and Carrie (AP Supervisor)
Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified
Close date pushed out from 31 December 2026 to 28 February 2027
Richard Hunsinger sent Kara Fisher a New Year connect request; no reply, initiative stalled on IT resources
Last recorded activity on the opportunity

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown, not absent. On the 1 October 2025 discovery call Julio Rivera confirmed David Yurman is 'talking to quite a few vendors' but named none, and the Salesforce Competitors field is empty. This is a greenfield replacement of a clunky in-house 2021 indirect-procurement tool, so the real near-term threat is a multi-vendor evaluation Zycus has no visibility into after 159 days of silence, compounded by the do-nothing risk that David Yurman keeps buying manually while IT capacity is elsewhere. Surfacing the shortlist and timeline is the first competitive task on re-engagement.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: On the 1 October 2025 call Richard Hunsinger positioned Zycus as aligned across every stated criterion, and Julio Rivera acknowledged Zycus 'checks a lot of the boxes.' Fit is strong on paper but unproven beyond a single pitch and unvalidated by a demo or the CFO.

best_practice_check.summary: Cold, single-threaded, greenfield deal that needs relationship revival before anything commercial. The win levers are re-opening Kara Fisher with value, multi-threading to the CFO sponsor, and converting confirmed pain into a quantified case.
flags: Re-open with a value trigger: send Kara Fisher a retail-peer P2P proof point to restart the stalled 2026 initiative before any commercial ask.
Multi-thread now: only operational AP/procurement contacts engaged; secure CFO sponsor Christopher Clipper and cultivate a real internal champion.
Convert pain to a business case: sign an NDA to unlock spend scope, then quantify savings and cycle-time impact with finance.

items.name: Christopher Clipper
items.title: Chief Financial Officer
items.risk: High: budget authority and sponsorship unconfirmed, never on a call
items.sentiment: Unknown; listed as Executive Sponsor but has never engaged in this cycle

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Kara Fisher led the 1 October 2025 discovery call and could have developed into one, but she has provided no advocacy, no internal introductions, and no reply to the 5 January 2026 outreach; the relationship is cooling toward dormant. Reactivating her or finding a new internal sponsor is the priority.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025; Next Step 5 January 2026; LastActivityDate 28 January 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic story landed on the 1 October 2025 call: Richard Hunsinger positioned Merlin Teams-based conversational intake and GenAI invoice capture in the high-90s, and Julio Rivera engaged on both (asking how the Teams agent is audited and confirming interest in pure-AI capture). It has not been re-landed since and no demo has proven it.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 7.0 / Moderate. The 1 October 2025 call supports genuine appetite: David Yurman wants a Teams-embedded conversational assistant and GenAI OCR at high-90s accuracy, both in their own requirements language, but the interest is rep-surfaced and early, not yet a buyer-driven mandate. The moderate rating fits.

items.detail: Initiative paused because David Yurman IT resources were pulled to other projects; brand-new indirect-procurement function has limited internal bandwidth
No champion and single-threaded to operational contacts; no one advocating internally to secure prioritization

items.detail: Do-nothing / status-quo risk: David Yurman keeps buying manually and never signs while IT capacity sits elsewhere, a no-decision loss rather than a competitive one

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has gone dark: one call 9 months ago, an unanswered January re-engagement, and 159 days of silence. The relationship is cooling toward dormant.

Stated IT-resource constraint blocking the initiative

critical_signals.text: The only real engagement, the 1 October 2025 discovery call, went well but nothing has moved since and the buyer has been silent for 159 days.
CFO Christopher Clipper is named as the executive sponsor but has never joined a call, so the budget owner is mapped yet completely unengaged.
David Yurman said in October they are evaluating several vendors but named none, so Zycus is competing blind and may already have been deprioritized.
No NDA is in place, so spend scope was withheld and no price, ROI, or funded budget has been established.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A greenfield David Yurman procure-to-pay deal that ran one strong discovery call on 1 October 2025 and then went dark for 159 days; the only thing standing between Zycus and momentum is a stalled, single-threaded relationship with no engaged economic buyer and no visibility into the competing vendors.

win_position.text: This sits low because after a strong 1 October 2025 discovery call where Julio Rivera and Kara Fisher named clear procure-to-pay pain, there has been no buyer contact since 28 January 2026, no champion, no engaged economic buyer, and no named competitor to anchor a win.

Kara Fisher, the indirect-procurement director and the only real relationship, has not replied to Richard Hunsinger's 5 January 2026 re-engagement note, and the initiative is stalled on David Yurman IT resourcing.
CFO Christopher Clipper is the listed executive sponsor but has never joined a call, so budget authority is unvalidated while Julio Rivera flagged an active multi-vendor evaluation on 1 October 2025.

deal_momentum.text: No forward movement in about 250 days in Qualified; the only 2026 touch is Richard Hunsinger's unanswered 5 January New Year note (source: Next Step and field history).
David Yurman paused the initiative because internal IT resources were pulled to other projects (Next Step, 5 January 2026), so momentum is buyer-blocked, not competitively lost.

customer_commitment.text: Interest was real but shallow: Julio Rivera closed the 1 October 2025 call saying Zycus 'definitely has a lot of the boxes we were thinking, really interesting food for thought,' yet declined to share spend scope with no NDA and booked no next meeting.
No NDA, no proposal request, and no second session; commitment has not advanced beyond an operational discovery call.

deal_risk.text: No champion: Kara Fisher engaged operationally but has shown no advocacy and gone silent for 159 days (LastActivityDate 28 January 2026).
The competitive field is unknown: Julio Rivera said on 1 October 2025 they are 'talking to quite a few vendors' but named none, so Zycus may have been deprioritized without notice.
Do-nothing is the primary threat: David Yurman can keep buying manually while IT capacity sits elsewhere, a no-decision loss rather than a loss to a rival.

champion_strength.evidence: No champion; Kara Fisher cooling and silent 159 days (Next Step 5 January 2026)

customer_preference.evidence: Positive but non-decisive on 1 October 2025; buyer evaluating multiple vendors, then went dark

deal_momentum.evidence: Stalled on IT resources; no activity since 28 January 2026

customer_preference.evidence: Julio Rivera on 1 October 2025: Zycus 'has a lot of the boxes we were thinking,' but 'we are talking to quite a few vendors' and just want to know 'what makes you different'; no Zycus-specific next step and dark since.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (manual processes, clunky 2021 tool, AP diverted to IT troubleshooting, 50-plus stores uncontrolled); no savings, headcount, or cycle-time figure and spend scope withheld pending NDA.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 51 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 51, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
